impacts.title: Advancement of European AI agent technology to global leadership position
impacts.description: MAESTRO's neuro-symbolic planning and formally verified coordination protocols establish a European-origin paradigm for trustworthy AI agents that differentiates from US/China approaches based on scale alone. The open-source framework enables European researchers and companies to build on these advances without dependency on non-European platforms.
impacts.expected_impact_alignment: Significant improvements in the autonomy, robustness and reliability of AI agents through advanced planning mechanisms, memory management, and reasoning capabilities.
indicators: ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in top AI venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI)
≥3 invited keynotes at major AI conferences
≥100 citations of MAESTRO publications within 3 years of publication
impacts.timeline: M12–M60 (publications from M12; citation impact measured 12 months post-project)

impacts.title: Enabling trustworthy AI deployment in European healthcare
impacts.description: The clinical decision support demonstrator establishes a validated pathway for AI agent deployment in healthcare, with auditable reasoning traces that support regulatory compliance (EU AI Act high-risk requirements). Dissemination through EDIHs enables hospital networks across Europe to adopt AI-assisted diagnostics.
impacts.expected_impact_alignment: Significant technological progress and innovation in Apply AI Strategy's prioritised sectors driven by challenge-oriented, AI-powered solutions.
indicators: ≥3 hospital networks engaged in post-project adoption planning by M48
Clinical validation report accepted by ≥1 national health technology assessment body
Demonstrable improvement in diagnostic throughput in pilot clinical settings

impacts.title: Accelerating AI-driven transformation in European manufacturing
impacts.description: The manufacturing demonstrator shows measurable quality and efficiency improvements achievable through multi-agent AI coordination in factory environments. Scaling through EIT Manufacturing and EDIH networks enables widespread adoption across European manufacturing SMEs.
impacts.expected_impact_alignment: Increase adoption of AI technologies across the following three key application domains: healthcare, advanced manufacturing (including AI-powered robotics) and in-vehicle autonomous driving.
indicators: ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings demonstrated in pilot factory
≥5 manufacturing SMEs engaged in technology transfer by M48
Technology transfer pathway established through ≥2 EDIHs

impacts.title: Demonstrating cross-sector transferability of European AI agent architecture
impacts.description: The logistics transfer demonstrator validates that the MAESTRO architecture — planning, memory, coordination, and tool orchestration — generalises beyond the call's specifically identified Apply AI sectors (healthcare, advanced manufacturing, in-vehicle autonomous driving) to additional high-value domains. This confirms the architecture's portability and strengthens the case for broader European economic impact. The architectural transferability analysis to in-vehicle autonomous driving further extends the demonstrated reach.
impacts.expected_impact_alignment: Increase competitiveness and visibility of the relevant AI community within key application domains, and promote collaborative approaches for AI development in these domains, fostering the ecosystem.
indicators: ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time demonstrated operationally in logistics
Pilot deployment in ≥3 international supply chain corridors
Architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving completed
Integration roadmap for ELI operational systems by M48

impacts.title: Strengthening the European AI innovation ecosystem
impacts.description: The open-source MAESTRO framework, benchmark suite, and AI-on-demand platform contributions create shared infrastructure that lowers barriers for European SMEs and startups to develop next-generation AI agent applications, reducing dependency on proprietary non-European AI agent platforms.
impacts.expected_impact_alignment: Increase competitiveness and visibility of the relevant AI community within key application domains, and promote collaborative approaches for AI development in these domains, fostering the ecosystem.
indicators: ≥500 GitHub stars on MAESTRO framework within 6 months of release
≥10 European SMEs/startups building on MAESTRO framework by M48
≥3 EDIHs actively disseminating MAESTRO technology
Benchmark suite adopted by ≥5 external research groups

impacts.title: Contribution to multi-agent coordination standards
impacts.description: MAESTRO's formally verified coordination protocols provide the basis for standardisation of multi-agent AI interaction, submitted to relevant standardisation bodies and aligned with emerging EU AI Act requirements for multi-agent system transparency.
impacts.expected_impact_alignment: Innovative multi-agent frameworks and protocols demonstrating effective decentralized coordination and collaboration among multiple AI agents beyond the capabilities of individual agents.
indicators: ≥1 standardisation proposal submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE
Protocol specification adopted by ≥3 external multi-agent research projects
Alignment report with EU AI Act multi-agent transparency requirements published
